//aliases: Claude Code tier aliases -> real Copilot model id. Dashed canonical ids make Claude Code label them correctly (e.g. 'Opus 4.8'). The [1m] suffix asks Claude Code to budget the 1M context window; the shim strips it before the request reaches Copilot.
//tierLabels: Optional display name/description for a tier row in Claude Code's /model picker (ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_<TIER>_MODEL_NAME/_DESCRIPTION). Used when a tier is repurposed to a non-obvious model (e.g. Fable -> a GPT model).

fable.name: GPT-5.6 Sol (1M)
fable.description: GPT-5.6 Sol via GitHub Copilot — 1M context

//customModelOption: One extra row added to Claude Code's /model picker via ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_MODEL_OPTION. Foundry mode supports a single custom option; additional models remain reachable by typing their id in /model. The [1m] suffix budgets the 1M window (stripped by the shim before Copilot).

customModelOption.name: GPT-5.6 Luna (1M)
customModelOption.description: GPT-5.6 Luna via GitHub Copilot — 1M context

//defaultModel: Session default written to the Claude `model` setting.
//responsesApiModels: Models that ONLY work on Copilot's /v1/responses endpoint (OpenAI Responses API). The shim translates Anthropic Messages <-> Responses for these.
//discovery: Curated model list exposed on GET /v1/models. `id` matches copilot-api's upstream (dotted) id; `canonical` is the dashed id Claude Code recognises. Non-claude ids are re-exposed under a synthetic 'anthropic-copilot-' prefix so Claude Code's gateway discovery keeps them. Discovery is OFF in Foundry mode (the default), so this is used only if you switch to gateway mode.
